purpose: Claude Code development standards for UiPath REFramework robot projects. Apply these rules whenever scaffolding, generating or reviewing UiPath project files.

framework.base: UiPath REFramework (state machine: Initialization → GetTransactionData → Process → EndProcess)
notes: Always use the MvR_REFramework as the default framework, not the plain UiPath REFramework.

The MvR framework adds: StartTime assignment in Initialization, a temporary config copy sequence above InitAllSettings to prevent file-lock errors during debugging, log mails (start + end), database logging support, and CreateAssets workflow.
Do not add logic to Main.xaml, SetTransactionStatus.xaml or other framework files. Process logic belongs in Process.xaml and invoked workflows.
State Machine is used only for the REFramework itself. All other workflows use Sequence (preferred) or Flowchart.

rules: No spaces in project names. Use CamelCase: e.g. Finance_AfrekenenContracten.
Avoid meaningless suffixes like 'framework'.
The project folder name matches the project name.
Do not include version numbers in the project name. Use Git branches/tags for versioning.
When making changes to a live robot, always work in a DEV branch. Never commit directly to Main.
GitHub repository naming: KlantNaam_RobotNaam.
Prefix each workflow file with a 3-digit sequence number.
Include the application name as the second part of the filename.
Do not rename workflow files after they are in use — it causes confusion in logs.
Group workflow files that belong to the same application in one subfolder under the project root.
For large application sets, use subfolders within the application folder.
Variable names must be meaningful and fully describe the entity they represent.
Use PascalCase (every word capitalised, no separators): TransactionNumber, FilePath, ReportName.
Maximum 20 characters. Abbreviate longer words if needed.
Short names (index, row, file) are acceptable for local/loop scopes.
Minimise variable scope to the smallest necessary sequence.
Do NOT use the Default Value field to initialise DataTables or Dictionaries — use an Assign activity instead.
variable_naming.boolean_convention: Boolean names must be self-describing so True/False is unambiguous. Example: bool_InlogschermExists. Use 'Not bool_X' rather than 'bool_X = False'.
variable_naming.dictionary_note: When using a Dictionary, maintain a Dictionaries.xlsx in the Documentation folder listing all keys.

rules: Apply the same naming rules as variables, plus the direction prefix.
Default values may be used for testing individual workflows, but must be cleared before linking to a calling workflow.
Always link arguments immediately when invoking a workflow and importing arguments.

Consider using a Dictionary argument instead of many individual arguments when many values must pass between workflows (pros: fewer arguments, easier to log; cons: less explicit, harder to debug in Locals panel).
Activity names must concisely describe the action taken. Keep the action verb: e.g. 'Click Opslaan button', 'Type Into Username field'.
The default UiPath-generated name is usually insufficient — rename every activity.
Assign and If activities especially always need a meaningful name.
When an activity deviates from default settings (e.g. uses a dynamic selector), add an Annotation explaining why.
Use the Comment activity and Annotations to explain complex or unusual logic for future maintainers.
Keep activities that operate on the same variable(s) close together; consider using Multi-Assign.
If an activity can throw an exception, its name will appear in the log — a good name makes triage much faster.
Sequences are preferred for all workflows. They are linear, easy to read and test independently.
Use Flowchart only when decision logic cannot be expressed cleanly in a Sequence, or when elements would need to be duplicated.
Keep Flowcharts small and never collapse elements inside them.
State Machine is reserved exclusively for the REFramework Main.xaml.
Break large workflows into smaller invoked sub-workflows so each can be tested independently.
Use mini-sequences within a workflow to visually group related activities.
Begin every significant sequence/workflow with a Log Message: 'In: WorkflowName'.
Combine duplicate logic into a single reusable workflow controlled by an in-argument (Boolean, selector info, etc.) rather than maintaining two copies.
Rename every If activity to describe the condition being evaluated.
Avoid deeply nested If statements — refactor to Flowchart or Switch when nesting becomes complex.
Use Switch Activity when branching on a single variable with multiple known values.
Use Flow Switch inside Flowcharts for the same purpose (supports 12+ cases).
Use Pick Branch when multiple parallel UI states may appear and you need to react to whichever appears first. Always include a Throw in the Element-did-not-appear branch.

storage_preference_order: 1. Never store credentials in plain text in workflows or Config files
rules: Retrieve credentials using the Get Credential activity, which returns String (username) and SecureString (password).
Use Type Secure Text for entering passwords into UI fields.
To convert SecureString to String when required by an activity: new System.Net.NetworkCredential("", SecurePassword).Password
Scope credential variables as narrowly as possible — never broader than needed.
Use Try-Catch for exceptions that need local handling before the Framework's own error handling takes over.
Multiple Catch blocks are allowed; only the most specific matching exception is caught.
The Finally block runs regardless of exception — use for mandatory cleanup.
Add Try-Catch in Process.xaml when needed. Never add extra logic to the existing Catch in Main.xaml.
Use Throw with BusinessRuleException for expected business failures: new BusinessRuleException('Description').
BusinessRuleException = no retry (same situation would recur). SystemException = retry eligible.
Use Rethrow inside a Catch block when you want to log or act on an exception but still propagate it to the Framework.
Error messages must never contain sensitive personal data.
Error messages must be clear, unique and traceable — but generic enough to be safe.
Use Retry Scope for sporadic transient failures (e.g. selector not found < 5% of runs) after other fixes are exhausted. Name the Retry Scope meaningfully since its error output is not specific.

For queue-based robots: use Framework MaxRetry for non-queue retry; use Queue retry settings for queue items. Consider Immediate Retry (MvR framework feature) for direct re-attempt without queue cycling.
Implement Point-of-no-Return logic when a transaction must not be retried after a certain step (e.g. after creating a record).

XAML only: Never generate a Catch block for generic Exception or System.Exception — these cause namespace resolution errors in UiPath Studio. Only catch BusinessRuleException explicitly. All other exceptions propagate to the Framework automatically.

sheets.Settings: Static values unlikely to change. Hard-coded or config-file stored. Examples: log messages, queue names.
sheets.Assets: Orchestrator asset names. Values managed in Orchestrator, not in the file itself.
sheets.Constants: Values that never change and have no benefit being in Orchestrator.

rules: Hard-code only truly static values (e.g. a fixed selector string).
Store values used in multiple places in Config.xlsx for single-point maintenance.
Use Orchestrator Assets for anything that may differ between DEV, TEST and PROD — allows migration without code changes.
Credential asset names should be stored as a regular Asset in Config so the flow can look up the asset name and then retrieve the credential dynamically.
The MvR framework includes a CreateAssets workflow that provisions assets from a 'Create asset' sheet in Config to multiple Orchestrator folders in one run.
A JSON-based config is an alternative to Excel (better version control and API integration, but less intuitive for non-developers).
Assets specific to one process go in that process's Orchestrator folder. Shared assets go in the root folder.

pre_populated_rows.Name: OrchestratorQueueName
pre_populated_rows.Description: Orchestrator Queue Name. Match with server.

pre_populated_rows.Name: logF_BusinessProcessName
pre_populated_rows.Description: Groups log data under one business process name

rules: OrchestratorQueueName: derive from PDD process name using Department_ProcessName convention.
logF_BusinessProcessName: use the process name from the PDD.
ConfigFileLocation and UseOrchestratorTransactions: always include with default values.

pre_populated_rows.Name: ProjectWithDispatcher
pre_populated_rows.Description: If the project has a dispatcher, then getqueueitems in GetTransactionData, otherwise AddTransactionItem

pre_populated_rows.Name: QueueRetry
pre_populated_rows.Description: If true, Orchestrator QueueRetry is used

pre_populated_rows.Name: MaxRetryNumber_FrameworkRetry
pre_populated_rows.Description: Must be 0 if working with Orchestrator queues

pre_populated_rows.Name: MaxConsecutiveSystemExceptions
pre_populated_rows.Description: Set to 0 to disable consecutive exception limit

pre_populated_rows.Name: LogMessage_GetTransactionDataError
pre_populated_rows.Value: Error getting transaction data for Transaction Number:

pre_populated_rows.Name: ExceptionMessage_ConsecutiveErrors
pre_populated_rows.Value: The maximum number of consecutive system exceptions was reached.

rules: Always include all default Constants rows — they are required by the MvR framework.
Set ProjectWithDispatcher to True only if the PDD describes a dispatcher/performer split.
Set QueueRetry to True only if the PDD explicitly requires Orchestrator queue retry.

columns: Description (Assets will always overwrite other config)

pre_populated_rows.Name: MaxTransactions
pre_populated_rows.Description: Must be integer, or INPUTDIALOG, or ALL (unlimited)

pre_populated_rows.Name: LogMessageAddress
pre_populated_rows.Description: A single dash will be converted to Nothing by the framework

rules: Name column: the key used in the config dictionary inside workflows.
Asset column: the exact Orchestrator asset name (follows AppName_Purpose convention).
Folder column: the Orchestrator folder path. Leave empty for process-default folder.
For every credential identified in the PDD: add one row with the asset name. The asset name is then used in the workflow to call Get Credential.
For every application URL, environment setting or configurable value in the PDD: add one row.
Asset naming: AppName_Purpose — e.g. SAP_Credential, SAP_URL, RXMission_ClientID.

pre_populated_rows.Name: LogMessageAddress
pre_populated_rows.Comments: A single dash will be converted to Nothing by the framework

rules: Every asset in the Assets sheet must also have a corresponding row in CreateAssets.
Credential assets: ValueType = Credential, Value = empty string (filled manually before first run), CreateAsset = True.
Text assets with a known default from the PDD: fill Value directly.
Text assets without a known value: use a placeholder like <<FILL_BEFORE_FIRST_RUN>>.
CreateAsset column: always True for new project scaffolding.
Asset Name must exactly match the Asset column value in the Assets sheet.
Comments: copy the Description from the Assets sheet for traceability.

LogMessageHtml.note: Pre-populate from PDD process name and department. Key fields: [var_subject], [var_procesnaam], [var_afzender].

pdd_derived_fields.source: derive from PDD process name: RPA-proces - <<ProcessName>>

LogmailAttachment.note: Add one row per transaction output field identified in the PDD. KeyTransactionDetails column = dictionary key name used in TransactionDetails in the workflow. Reserved words that cannot be used as column names: Transactienummer, Status, Toelichting, TransactionID.

rules: Begin every important workflow/sequence with: Log Message — 'In: WorkflowName' at Info level.
Use Trace level for intermediate debug log messages so they do not pollute normal Info logs.
Log messages must contain enough context to identify the transaction and state.
NEVER log sensitive personal data in Orchestrator Cloud log messages.
Never use queue item Reference or Specific Content fields for personal data.
The MvR framework supports optional SQL database logging. Activate by setting asset Db_UseConnection = true and providing Db_GetCredentialsAssetName.
Database table additions must follow the existing schema: [NameVariable] [typevariable](255) NULL.
Begin with the application or category name.
Group by application. Shared assets (credentials, URLs used by multiple processes) go in the root Orchestrator folder.
Process-specific assets go in the process's own Orchestrator subfolder.
Typically one queue per process, same name as the process.
Queue names appear in dashboards — keep them meaningful.
Create queues in the correct department subfolder in Orchestrator.

input_method_preference: 3. Hardware events (most compatible, requires visible window)
selector_rules: Selectors must work consistently across DEV, TEST and PROD environments and regardless of logged-in username.
Replace dynamic/variable parts of attribute values with wildcards (*).
Remove attributes whose entire value is a wildcard — they add no selectivity.
Avoid idx except for small stable values (1 or 2).
Use aaname (accessible name) where possible — it is human-readable and helps interpret logs.
Add containers (Attach Browser, Attach Window) to scope partial selectors. This is faster than full selectors and easier to maintain.
Use Anchor or Find Relative Element when a direct selector cannot be made reliable.
Use fuzzy selectors for elements where minor UI changes (capitalisation, typos) are expected, but verify accuracy with a subsequent Find Element.
Annotate any non-standard selector with an explanation.
synchronisation: Prefer Check App State (modern) over Element Exists for conditional UI waits — it combines the check with True/False branches.
Use Find Element (classic) to block until an element appears and throw a SystemException if it does not — equivalent to a mandatory wait.
Element Exists never throws — use only when the absence of an element is an acceptable outcome.
Add Verify Execution to Click/TypeInto activities as a replacement for Find Element + Retry Scope where applicable.
Use Pick Branch for parallel UI state detection to avoid timeout overhead on the happy flow.
Use DelayMS sparingly — prefer explicit element waits.
background_automation: For unattended robots sharing a desktop: use SimulateType, SimulateClick and SendWindowMessages.
Use SetText, Check and SelectItem for background data entry.
GetText, GetFullText and WebScraping work in background.
Consider Picture-in-Picture (PiP) if the environment supports it.

rules: One repository per robot.
Work in a DEV branch when the robot is live. Never commit directly to Main while in production.
Push intermediate versions as commits rather than using folder version numbers.
Project folder name stays constant — versioning is managed through Git, not the folder name.
Reusable workflow components are stored in the MvR library.
Any value that could vary between clients or environments must be an in-argument — never hard-coded.
Library components must be generically built so they work across different clients using the same application.
When a process step matches a library component, always invoke the library component rather than rebuilding from scratch.

options_in_preference_order: Office 365 Outlook (Graph API — most modern, works with App Id & Secret or modern auth)
Exchange (direct server connection — stable, needs credentials and server details)
Outlook Desktop activities (legacy, avoid if possible — can be unstable)
log_mail_rules: Send a start mail to the key user for robots that trigger automatically, for critical processes, or when the key user needs to know timing.
Send a business end-mail to the key user with process results.
Send a separate management end-mail (beheer mail) to administrators — must not contain personal data.
Never include personal/sensitive data in any log mail, even to key users.
Customise mail appearance (background, footer, robot name, subject) via Config where possible.

rules: Never log sensitive personal data in Orchestrator Cloud (log messages, queue item reference, queue item specific content, assets).
Never store credentials in plain text in workflows or config files.
Credential variables must have the minimum necessary scope.

When recording process sessions for documentation, ensure test data contains no real personal information. Use black rectangles (not blur) to redact sensitive data in screenshots — blur is reversible.

required_fields: projectId must be a valid non-empty GUID (xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx). Generate a fresh random GUID when scaffolding — never leave it as an empty string.
entryPoints[0].uniqueId must be a valid non-empty GUID. Generate a fresh random GUID when scaffolding — never leave it as an empty string.
runtimeOptions.isAttended must reflect the process type from the PDD (false for unattended robots).
project_json_rules.guid_generation: Always produce two distinct random GUIDs when scaffolding project.json: one for projectId and one for entryPoints[0].uniqueId. Use PowerShell ([System.Guid]::NewGuid().ToString()) or any other method. Never copy GUIDs from another project.json and never leave them as empty strings.

process_xaml_scaffolding.description: Process.xaml must be generated as fully as possible from the PDD/SDD. Do not put TODO placeholders where actual structure can be derived. Sub-workflow stubs carry the TODOs for implementation; Process.xaml carries the per-transaction orchestration logic.

init_vs_process_split.rule: Open/login workflows belong in Framework/InitAllApplications.xaml — NOT in Process.xaml. InitAllApplications runs once per robot run (Initialization state). Process.xaml runs once per transaction item.
init_vs_process_split.init_applications_workflows: Any workflow that opens an application (browser, SAP, desktop app) or logs into an application is invoked from Framework/InitAllApplications.xaml. Typical naming: NNN_AppName_OpenEnInloggen.xaml or NNN_AppName_Login.xaml.
init_vs_process_split.process_workflows: All per-transaction business logic steps — steps that act on a specific queue item or transaction — go in Process.xaml.
init_vs_process_split.how_to_classify: If a step happens once per run regardless of how many transactions there are → InitAllApplications. If it repeats for every transaction item → Process.xaml.
init_vs_process_split.process_annotation_note: Add a note to the Process.xaml root annotation when a login/open step has been moved to InitAllApplications: 'NB: [AppName] openen en inloggen ([filename]) wordt uitgevoerd vanuit Framework/InitAllApplications.xaml — niet hier.'

rules: Root Sequence: annotation summarising the process name, numbered flow steps derived from PDD, and uitvalpad (BRE path) if present. Set IsAnnotationDocked: True.

Variables block: declare all data variables that flow between sub-workflows. Derive names and types from the PDD (e.g. BSN as String, bool_IsClientBekend as Boolean). Use type prefixes per variable_naming rules.
First activity: LogMessage at Info level — 'In: Process - ProjectName'.
Wrap the main flow in a TryCatch with DisplayName 'TryCatch - Process'.
Try block: Sequence DisplayName 'Try - Hoofdproces' containing one InvokeWorkflowFile per process step in PDD order.

Each InvokeWorkflowFile: DisplayName = 'NNN - Description (AppName)', WorkflowFileName = correct relative path, Arguments = empty Dictionary stub with x:TypeArguments="x:String, Argument" (NOT x:String, x:Object — that causes a type error in Studio), Annotation = 'TODO: Wire arguments' followed by each expected in_/out_/io_ argument and the local variable it maps to.
Conditional logic from PDD: generate If or nested If activities with Condition derived from a bool_ variable. Place the relevant InvokeWorkflowFile calls in Then/Else branches.
Final activity in Try block: LogMessage at Info level logging key result variables to confirm success.

Catch block: catch ui:BusinessRuleException only. Contents: LogMessage Warn with exception.Message, then InvokeWorkflowFile for any error-handling workflow identified in the PDD (with argument wiring TODO annotation), then Rethrow.
Never add a Catch for generic Exception or System.Exception.

XML comments (<!-- Stap N: ... -->) may be used inside Process.xaml as structural separators between invoke steps — they are for the XAML source reader, not the designer. They are acceptable here because the InvokeWorkflowFile annotations serve the Studio developer.

sdd_generation.description: Rules for generating a Solution Design Document (SDD) from a PDD using the /solutions skill. The SDD is the authoritative input for /scaffold-robot — scaffold-robot reads the SDD and does not fall back to the PDD.
sdd_generation.output_format: Three files are produced per SDD run: (1) Markdown .md — machine-readable, parsed by /scaffold-robot; (2) JSON _data.json — intermediate data file used by the Python script; (3) Word .docx — human-readable deliverable for stakeholders, generated by Scripts/generate_sdd_docx.py using python-docx. The .md is the authoritative source for scaffolding; the .docx is for review and sign-off.
sdd_generation.output_language: Dutch — all headings, prose and table labels must be in Dutch.
sdd_generation.pdd_location: Documentation/ at the repo root — PDDs are always placed here before running /solutions.
sdd_generation.output_path: {ProjectName}/Documentation/ — SDD files are written inside the automation project folder. The project folder may already exist when /solutions runs; if not, /solutions creates it together with its Documentation/ subfolder.
sdd_generation.placeholder_value: <<ONBEKEND>> — use for any field that cannot be determined from the PDD.
critical_rules: in_Config must always be listed as an Input argument for every workflow stub in Section 4.N.1 — it is required by the MvR framework for every invoked workflow.
in_TransactionItem (QueueItem) must be added as an input argument for every stub in queue-based robots.
Queue naam defaults to Projectnaam (Department_ProcessName) if not explicitly named in the PDD.
Projectnaam must always follow Department_ProcessName CamelCase — never spaces, never version numbers.
The asset table in Section 2.3 is the single authoritative source for all Config.xlsx Assets and CreateAssets rows — scaffold-robot reads only this table, not the PDD.
If any field required by scaffold-robot is <<ONBEKEND>>, scaffold-robot must surface it in its pre-flight summary and ask for resolution before generating files.

LOGIN RULE: For every unique application in the process, a login/open workflow must be the first automated step for that application. Add it explicitly in Section 1.2 Procesflow as a 'Geautomatiseerd' row with Sub-proces = 'OpenEnInloggen' (or 'Inloggen' if the app is already open). It must also appear as a Section 4.N entry with a corresponding stub in scaffold-robot. Never assume login is handled elsewhere — it must always be an explicit workflow stub per application.

sections.heading: Versie- en revisietabel
initial_row: Eerste concept op basis van PDD

sections.heading: Stakeholders
sections.scaffold_robot_rule: Opdrachtgever and Proceseigenaar (Naam Klant column) → README header.

rows.scaffold_robot_uses: logF_BusinessProcessName in Config Settings; LogMessageHtml var_subject and var_procesnaam

rows.pdd_source: Department_ProcessName CamelCase derived from Afdeling + Procesnaam
rows.scaffold_robot_uses: project folder name; project.json name field; OrchestratorQueueName default

rows.pdd_source: department reference in PDD title or scope

sections.heading: 1.2 Procesflow
sections.pdd_source: process flow / swimlane diagram / step overview in PDD
rules: One row per major process step.
Nr. must be sequential integers starting at 1.
Handmatig / Geautomatiseerd: use exactly 'Geautomatiseerd' or 'Handmatig' — scaffold-robot filters on this exact value.
Voorganger: the Nr. of the preceding step, or '-' for the first step.
sections.scaffold_robot_rule: Rows where 'Handmatig / Geautomatiseerd' = 'Geautomatiseerd' become workflow stubs. Filename = zero-pad(Nr., 3) + '_' + Applicatie (no spaces) + '_' + Sub-proces (PascalCase, no spaces) + '.xaml'. Nr. also sets the 3-digit prefix on InvokeWorkflowFile DisplayName in Process.xaml.

sections.heading: 1.3 Decompositie Processtappen
rules: Include a numbered list of all automated steps matching Nr. and Sub-proces from 1.2.
Include the uitvalpad (BRE scenario) as a separate bullet at the end.
This numbered list becomes the annotation text on the Process.xaml root Sequence.
sections.scaffold_robot_rule:
Numbered list items (joined with 
) → Process.xaml root Sequence sap2010:Annotation.AnnotationText. Uitvalpad bullet → appended after the numbered list.

sections.heading: 1.4 Benodigde rechten, applicaties en functionaliteiten
sections.pdd_source: system/application and access/permissions sections in PDD

subsections.heading: 1.4.1 Browser
subsections.content: Specify required browser (e.g. Edge) and any relevant browser settings such as automatic download location.

sections.scaffold_robot_rule: Browser column value → selector strategy note appended to the stub annotation for that application's workflows.

rows.pdd_source: queue name from PDD or derived as Projectnaam

sections.heading: 2.2 Queue en retry mechanisme
sections.pdd_source: exception handling / uitvalscenario section in PDD
sections.scaffold_robot_rule: BusinessRuleException rows (Trigger column) → Catch block sap2010:Annotation.AnnotationText in Process.xaml; also appended to relevant stub annotations.

sections.heading: 2.3 Init-fase

default_rows.Omschrijving: Must be integer, or INPUTDIALOG, or ALL (unlimited)
A single dash will be converted to Nothing by the framework

sections.pdd_source: credential/access section and config/environment section in PDD
rules: Add one row per Credential identified in the PDD: Type = Credential, Waarde = (leeg).
Add one row per URL, environment setting or configurable value: Type = Text, Waarde = known value or <<FILL_BEFORE_FIRST_RUN>>.
Asset naming follows AppName_Purpose convention: e.g. SAP_Credential, Suite_URL.
Always include the 4 default framework rows listed in default_rows.
This table is the ONLY source scaffold-robot uses for Config.xlsx Assets and CreateAssets sheets.
sections.scaffold_robot_rule: Every row → one row in Config.xlsx Assets sheet (Name = Asset naam, Asset = Asset naam, Folder = '', Description = Omschrijving) AND one row in CreateAssets sheet (Name = Asset naam, ValueType = Type, Value = Waarde, CreateAsset = True, Comments = Omschrijving).

sections.heading: 2.4 GetTransactionData-fase
sections.pdd_source: transaction data / queue retrieval description in PDD

sections.heading: 2.5 Procesfase
sections.pdd_source: process phase / main flow description in PDD

sections.heading: 2.6 Eindprocesfase
sections.pdd_source: end process / cleanup description in PDD

sections.heading: 3 Omgevingsafhankelijkheden
sections.pdd_source: environment / test vs prod / infrastructure section in PDD

sections.heading: 4.N Stap N: [Sub-proces] ([Applicatie])
sections.type: repeating — one sub-section per Geautomatiseerd row in 1.2
sections.rule: Generate one 4.N section per row in Section 1.2 where 'Handmatig / Geautomatiseerd' = 'Geautomatiseerd'. N matches the Nr. from that row.

rows.value: NNN_AppName_Action.xaml — must match exactly the filename derived from 1.2
rows.scaffold_robot_uses: exact filename for stub file creation
AppName part of filename and annotation
first line of stub annotation text (after filename)

rows.pdd_source: how to reach the starting screen for this step

rules: Always include in_Config (Dictionary<String,Object>)
Always include in_TransactionItem (QueueItem) for queue-based robots
rows.scaffold_robot_uses: Input argument list in stub annotation 'Wire arguments' section
Output argument list in stub annotation 'Wire arguments' section

sub_sections.heading: 4.N.2 Omschrijving handelingen
sub_sections.pdd_source: step-by-step actions for this process step from PDD work instructions
sub_sections.scaffold_robot_rule:
Numbered list items → numbered TODO steps in the stub annotation (
-separated, each prefixed with step number). These become the concrete implementation instructions the developer sees in UiPath Studio.

claude_code_instructions.scaffolding: Follow .claude/commands/scaffold-robot.md for the full step-by-step scaffolding procedure. That file is the authoritative source — do not derive scaffolding steps from this file.
claude_code_instructions.sdd_generation: Follow .claude/commands/solutions.md for the full SDD generation procedure. That file is the authoritative source. The sdd_generation section in this file defines the SDD structure and section-to-scaffold mapping.
claude_code_instructions.xaml_generation: See xaml_generation_rules section below — that is the canonical source for all XAML generation rules.

critical_rules: NEVER reference Exception types using the x: XAML namespace prefix. Exception lives in the System namespace, not the XAML x: namespace.
NEVER generate a generic Catch(Exception) block in XAML. Unhandled exceptions bubble up naturally to the REFramework, which handles retry logic. Do not try to catch them explicitly.
In Process.xaml, only catch BusinessRuleException explicitly. All other exceptions should propagate to the Framework.

BusinessRuleException is referenced as: ui:BusinessRuleException where ui is xmlns:ui="http://schemas.uipath.com/workflow/activities". Never use clr-namespace:UiPath.Core;assembly=UiPath.Core — it does not resolve in current UiPath Studio versions.
When generating Try-Catch in XAML, always verify the Catch type resolves to a fully qualified .NET type via the correct namespace alias, never via x: or bare type names.
If in doubt about a type reference in XAML, omit the Catch block entirely rather than generating an invalid reference. The Framework safety net is sufficient.
Do not generate Catch blocks for System.Exception, System.ApplicationException or any generic base exception class — these cause namespace resolution errors in UiPath Studio.
Only generate Catch blocks you are certain will resolve correctly (e.g. BusinessRuleException). Leave a TODO comment for any other exception handling the developer should add in UiPath Studio.
xaml_generation_rules.valid_catch_example: Catch x:TypeArguments="ui:BusinessRuleException" — where ui namespace is declared as xmlns:ui="http://schemas.uipath.com/workflow/activities". This is the only correct alias. NEVER use clr-namespace:UiPath.Core;assembly=UiPath.Core for BusinessRuleException — it does not resolve in current UiPath Studio versions.
invalid_patterns_to_avoid: Any bare type name in a generic type argument inside XAML without a proper CLR namespace alias — INVALID

rules: NEVER use XML comments (<!-- TODO -->) for developer instructions. XML comments are invisible in UiPath Studio's designer and provide no value to the developer working in Studio.
Every stub activity gets a meaningful DisplayName describing what the activity does. Never put [TODO] or any placeholder text in the DisplayName.
Put TODO instructions in sap2010:Annotation.AnnotationText on the activity — both on the workflow root Sequence and on individual placeholder Sequences inside it.
Always set IsAnnotationDocked: True in the ViewState of any annotated element so the annotation opens automatically when the developer opens the file.
Annotation text structure: 1) workflow file name (root only), 2) Doel (purpose from PDD), 3) Input/Output arguments (root only), 4) numbered TODO steps the developer must complete in UiPath Studio.

Use 
 for newlines and " for double-quotes inside annotation attribute values.
Derive the numbered TODO steps directly from the PDD process description — be specific about which activities, selectors and config keys are involved.
The sap2010 namespace must be declared on the root Activity element: xmlns:sap2010="http://schemas.microsoft.com/netfx/2010/xaml/activities/presentation".

xaml_example.description: Correct pattern: meaningful DisplayName on every activity, TODO instructions in the annotation
xaml_example.root_sequence:
<Sequence DisplayName="001_Suite_Login" sap2010:Annotation.AnnotationText="001_Suite_Login

Doel: Log in op Suite via Edge.

Input:
  in_Config → configuratiewoordenboek
